subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Activated Charcoal Explained
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Activated Charcoal Explained.

hook.text: In 1831, a professor publicly swallowed strychnine with charcoal—and walked away unharmed.

buildup.text: Activated charcoal has a massive surface area—one gram has the area of a tennis court.

discovery.text: Toxins bind to charcoal's surface through adsorption, preventing absorption into the blood.

twist.text: It must be given within 1-2 hours of ingestion and doesn't work on all poisons.

climax.text: It's ineffective against alcohols, metals, acids, and alkalis—know its limits.

punchline.text: Charcoal traps poison like a sponge. But timing is everything.

quiz.question: How does activated charcoal prevent poisoning?
options: Toxins bind to its surface through adsorption
